arguments.argument: The proposal to optimize resource allocation towards "high-performing" regions may seem appealing from a business perspective, but it would undoubtedly perpetuate existing inequalities and create new ones. By prioritizing students from regions with higher completion rates and engagement metrics, we would be reinforcing the notion that some students are more deserving of access to quality education than others.
